heroHeadline: PLA Sorties Drop to 6 on April 22 After 24-Sortie Spike; PLAN Formation Passes Okinawa Completing Tit-for-Tat Arc — 22 Days to Trump-Xi Summit
heroSubtitle: Taiwan's MND detected 6 PLA aircraft sorties and 5 PLAN vessels on April 22 — a sharp pullback from the 24-sortie surge on April 21 (highest non-exercise count since Justice Mission-2025 in December 2025), with 4 of 6 sorties crossing the median line. Simultaneously, PLA Navy Formation 133 passed through Japan's Okinawa islands (Yonaguni-Iriomote Waterway) on its return from Western Pacific exercises, completing the full Chinese military response arc to JMSDF destroyer Ikazuchi's April 17 Taiwan Strait transit: East China Sea patrols (Apr 18) → Western Pacific exercises with Liaoning carrier (Apr 19-21) → Okinawa passage home (Apr 22). The de-escalation follows the established spike-then-pullback pattern (Apr 21: 24 sorties → Apr 22: 6 sorties), consistent with previous sequences (Apr 11: 17 sorties → Apr 12: 2 sorties). With 22 days to the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing (May 14-15), analysts assess Beijing is sustaining baseline median-line pressure while calibrating against another diplomatic liability surge. Xi called for 'prudence on US arms sales to Taiwan' in his last call with Trump; the $14B PAC-3/NASAMS package remains held pending the summit. Taiwan's NT$1.25T special defense budget remains in legislative negotiations.
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, CNBC, MarineLink, Stars and Stripes, Japan News Pravda, US News & World Report, Free Malaysia Today, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
